name: StegoForge
description: The ultimate steganography and digital forensics toolkit. Hide and extract data across images, audio, video, documents, and network packets, or run 11 advanced detection engines to uncover hidden payloads.
readme_summary: StegoForge is a comprehensive Python toolkit for steganography and digital forensics. It allows users to hide and extract data within various file types like images, audio, and video, while also providing advanced detection engines to uncover hidden information. The tool is designed for security researchers, CTF players, and digital forensics practitioners.
key_features: Data embedding and extraction across images, audio, video, documents, and network packets
AES-256-GCM encryption for payloads
11 advanced steganalysis detection engines
Standalone, zero-dependency binaries for Windows, macOS, and Linux
Batch processing for embedding data into multiple files
Capacity and stealth score analysis for carrier files
Simulation of platform recompression (e.g., Twitter) to test payload survival
Local web UI for an interactive experience
CTF mode for quick forensic analysis
primary_use_case: To provide a unified and powerful platform for both hiding (steganography) and finding (digital forensics) hidden data within various digital media, catering to security professionals and enthusiasts.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan, depicted as a digital sculptor, is meticulously weaving intricate patterns of glowing data streams into the surface of a seemingly ordinary, large stone statue representing a digital image. The data streams are subtly flowing from a small, open toolbox labeled 'StegoForge'. The statue's surface shows faint, almost imperceptible geometric patterns where the data is embedded. In the background, a magnifying glass hovers over a section of the statue, revealing a hidden compartment within the stone. The overall mood is one of quiet, precise creation and discovery, with a clean, modern aesthetic and a color palette of deep blues and glowing golds. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with high detail.

name: pxpipe
description: cut Fable 5 token usage by rendering text context as images
readme_summary: pxpipe is a local proxy that reduces token usage for large language models like Claude by rendering bulky text context (like system prompts, tool documentation, and conversation history) into images. Since image token cost is based on pixel dimensions rather than content, this significantly cuts down on the total tokens required for a request, leading to substantial cost savings.
key_features: Reduces LLM token usage by converting text context to images
Fixed token cost for images regardless of text volume
Local proxy intercepts and rewrites requests before sending to LLM
Significant cost savings on LLM API calls
Dashboard for monitoring token savings, conversions, and settings
Configurable model support and lossy conversion handling
primary_use_case: To drastically reduce the token count and associated costs of interacting with large language models by encoding dense text context into image format.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a vast library. Instead of books, the shelves are filled with dense scrolls of text. The librarian is shown using a magical magnifying glass that transforms these scrolls into compact, vibrant picture frames, each containing a miniature, detailed scene representing the scroll's content. The background shows a bustling digital cityscape with subtle glowing data streams. Small UI elements displaying token counts and savings are visible on a nearby console. The overall mood is one of efficient knowledge compression and cost reduction. Rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, modern aesthetic and a color palette of blues, greens, and golds.

name: romm
description: A beautiful, powerful, self-hosted rom manager and player.
readme_summary: RomM is a self-hosted ROM manager and player that allows users to scan, organize, and play their game collection through a web interface. It enriches game libraries with metadata and artwork from various online sources and supports multiple platforms and custom tagging.
key_features: Scan and enhance game library with metadata from IGDB, Screenscraper, and MobyGames
Fetch custom artwork from SteamGridDB
Display achievements from Retroachievements
Support for 400+ platforms
Play games directly from the browser using EmulatorJS and RuffleRS
Share library with limited access and permissions
Official apps for Playnite, Android, and CFWs
Support for multi-disk games, DLCs, mods, hacks, patches, and manuals
Parse and filter by tags in filenames
View, upload, update, and delete games from any modern web browser
primary_use_case: Organizing and playing a personal retro game collection through a centralized, web-based interface.
cover_image_prompt: A magical, ornate library where ancient scrolls (representing ROM files) are being automatically sorted and cataloged by glowing, ethereal hands. Each scroll transforms into a beautifully illustrated book with vibrant cover art (metadata and artwork) as it's placed on a shelf. Small, holographic screens nearby display game titles and icons (game library interface). The repository name 'RomM' is subtly etched into a grand archway in the background. The scene is bathed in a warm, inviting light, with a touch of digital sparkle. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with rich textures and a fantasy-tech aesthetic.

name: no-mistakes
readme_summary: This Go-based tool acts as a local Git proxy, intercepting pushes to a remote repository. It spins up a disposable worktree to run an AI-driven validation pipeline, automatically fixing minor issues and escalating complex ones for user approval before finally pushing to the configured target and opening a clean Pull Request.
key_features: Local git proxy for push operations
AI-driven validation pipeline (review, test, docs, lint)
Disposable worktree for non-blocking operations
Automatic safe fixes and human-escalated complex fixes
Agent-agnostic support (Claude, Codex, Copilot, etc.)
Automatic clean PR creation upon successful validation
primary_use_case: To automate and enhance the code review process by ensuring code quality and correctness before it reaches the main repository through AI-powered checks and automated fixes, resulting in cleaner Pull Requests.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous gatekeeper, depicted as a wise owl wearing a small, glowing headset, stands before a pristine, golden portal representing a Git repository. The owl is examining a series of small, glowing code snippets and documents that are being fed into the portal. Some snippets are automatically being polished by the owl's gentle, glowing talons, while others are being flagged with a soft red light for a human hand (partially visible) to review. Beyond the portal, a clean, organized landscape of merged code branches is visible. The scene is set in a serene, digital library with subtle circuit board patterns on the walls, illuminated by a soft, ethereal glow. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a color palette of blues, golds, and soft whites.

name: orca
description: Orca is the ADE for working with a fleet of parallel agents. Run any coding agent with your own subscription. Available on desktop and mobile.
readme_summary: Orca is an AI-powered IDE designed for orchestrating multiple parallel coding agents. It allows users to run various AI models like Codex and ClaudeCode simultaneously, manage their outputs in separate worktrees, and monitor progress via a mobile companion app.
key_features: Mobile Companion App (iOS/Android) for monitoring and steering agents
Parallel Worktrees for running multiple agents in isolated Git worktrees
Advanced Terminal Splits with WebGL rendering and persistent scrollback
Design Mode for capturing UI elements (HTML, CSS, screenshots) for agent prompts
Support for multiple AI coding agents (Codex, ClaudeCode, OpenCode, Pi)
primary_use_case: Orchestrating and managing multiple AI coding agents in parallel for development tasks, allowing for comparison and selection of the best results.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor leading a symphony orchestra, where each musician represents a different AI coding agent (Codex, ClaudeCode, etc.). The conductor, embodying Orca, gestures to guide their parallel efforts, with musical notes transforming into lines of code and architectural blueprints. Each agent is in its own distinct, illuminated work area. Subtle UI elements showing progress bars and agent status are visible on a floating screen. The overall mood is collaborative and productive, set against a backdrop of a futuristic, organized workspace. The style should be a vibrant 3D isometric illustration with clear iconography and a clean, modern aesthetic.

name: headunit-revived
description: Headunit App for displaying Android Auto
readme_summary: Headunit Revived is an Android application that transforms an Android tablet or phone into an Android Auto receiver. It allows users to mirror their phone's Android Auto interface onto a larger screen, enhancing the in-car entertainment and navigation experience.
key_features: Turns Android tablets/phones into Android Auto receivers
Supports wired USB connections
Offers wireless connection options (Helper Mode, Wireless Launcher, Native)
Provides an Intent-based connection trigger for automation
Includes troubleshooting tips for common issues like Google Maps interaction and connection drops
primary_use_case: To enable users to use Android Auto on a secondary Android device, such as a tablet, by turning it into a headunit receiver.
cover_image_prompt: A split scene: on the left, a modern car dashboard with a sleek, integrated tablet displaying a vibrant Android Auto interface. On the right, a person holding a smartphone, with digital streams of navigation, music, and app icons flowing from the phone towards the tablet on the dashboard. The tablet screen shows these elements clearly rendered, acting as a larger, more accessible display. Subtle glowing lines connect the phone to the tablet, symbolizing the data transfer. The overall mood is seamless integration and enhanced connectivity. The style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a bright, futuristic color palette, emphasizing the 'revived' and improved user experience.
